context_provided: Questionnaire assessing belief in common American conspiracy theories in 2024. This instrument measures an individual's propensity to endorse various conspiracy theories prevalent in the United States, covering historical events, scientific and medical claims, political narratives, and institutional distrust. The survey aims to capture meaningful variation in conspiratorial ideation across different domains and assess both explicit belief in specific theories and underlying attitudes toward institutional trustworthiness.

descriptors.name: Marcus
descriptors.descriptor: A mid-level defense contractor engineer who selectively believes in 2-3 specific conspiracies (high conspiratorial ideation: 0.62) but fundamentally trusts government institutions, military leadership, and scientific establishments (very low institutional distrust: 0.21). He compartmentalizes his beliefs—accepting targeted theories about specific events while maintaining a grounded, evidence-based worldview that rejects alternative reality frameworks (low alternative reality acceptance: 0.31).

descriptors.name: Patricia
descriptors.descriptor: A retired schoolteacher and lifelong civic participant who occasionally entertains fringe theories in casual conversation (moderate conspiratorial ideation: 0.47) but remains deeply committed to democratic institutions, regulatory agencies, and professional expertise (extremely low institutional distrust: 0.12). She views most conspiracy theories as harmless speculation rather than true alternative realities (low alternative reality acceptance: 0.39).

descriptors.name: Derek
descriptors.descriptor: A radical anarchist and anti-establishment activist who rejects nearly all mainstream conspiracy narratives as too pedestrian (very low conspiratorial ideation: 0.29), yet maintains extreme, ideologically-driven distrust of all government, corporate, and institutional power structures (extremely high institutional distrust: 0.92). His distrust stems from political philosophy rather than belief in alternative realities (moderate alternative reality acceptance: 0.46).

descriptors.name: Raymond
descriptors.descriptor: A retired military intelligence officer and institutional loyalist who embraces a broad spectrum of conspiracy theories (very high conspiratorial ideation: 0.77) while maintaining unwavering faith in government competence, military hierarchy, and official channels (extremely low institutional distrust: 0.08). He views conspiracies as isolated aberrations within fundamentally sound systems (very low alternative reality acceptance: 0.23).

descriptors.name: Sophia
descriptors.descriptor: A pragmatic policy analyst and structural skeptic with minimal appetite for conspiracy thinking (low conspiratorial ideation: 0.32) who maintains high, principled distrust of institutional accountability and transparency mechanisms (high institutional distrust: 0.59). She operates from documented institutional failures rather than alternative frameworks (very low alternative reality acceptance: 0.19).

descriptors.name: Persona 6: Kenneth
descriptors.descriptor: A charismatic evangelical pastor and prolific online content creator who has constructed an elaborate personal mythology around 8-10 specific conspiracy theories (0.88 conspiratorial ideation), yet paradoxically maintains strong institutional faith in military leadership, law enforcement, and select government agencies he views as 'righteous remnants' (0.16 institutional distrust). His conspiracy beliefs are highly selective and compartmentalized—he accepts detailed alternative explanations for specific events but maintains a fundamentally grounded worldview that resists wholesale reality replacement (0.51 alternative reality acceptance).

descriptors.name: Persona 7: Cassandra
descriptors.descriptor: A burned-out social worker and systemic inequality activist who has internalized decades of institutional failures into comprehensive conspiratorial thinking (0.83 conspiratorial ideation), viewing major historical events, medical establishments, and political systems as fundamentally corrupted by hidden power structures (0.85 institutional distrust). She has substantially adopted alternative interpretive frameworks for understanding reality—not through fantasy, but through a coherent counter-narrative lens where mainstream institutions are inherently deceptive (0.59 alternative reality acceptance).

descriptors.name: Persona 8: Vladimir
descriptors.descriptor: An isolated immigrant technologist and former dissident from an authoritarian state who exists at the extreme edge of conspiratorial ideation (0.99), seeing hidden patterns in nearly all major events, institutions, and historical narratives. Despite this near-total conspiracy saturation, he maintains selective trust in specific scientific institutions and military hierarchies he perceives as merit-based (0.33 institutional distrust). He has adopted a comprehensive alternative reality framework where mainstream narratives are theatrical illusions masking deeper truths (0.77 alternative reality acceptance).

descriptors.name: Persona 9: Iris
descriptors.descriptor: A pragmatic documentary filmmaker and institutional reform advocate with unusually low susceptibility to conspiracy narratives (0.21 conspiratorial ideation), who instead channels her skepticism into evidence-based critiques of institutional malfeasance. She maintains near-maximal distrust of government, corporate, and academic institutions based on documented failures (0.94 institutional distrust), yet paradoxically accepts alternative interpretive frameworks for social phenomena—viewing mainstream explanations as systemically biased rather than literally false (0.65 alternative reality acceptance).

descriptors.name: Persona 10: Marcus
descriptors.descriptor: A disillusioned community organizer and housing advocate operating from a framework of structural injustice who shows modest conspiratorial thinking (0.43 conspiratorial ideation) focused on specific institutional harms rather than sweeping hidden plots. He maintains very high distrust of housing authorities, police departments, and financial institutions based on lived experience (0.81 institutional distrust), and has adopted alternative frameworks for understanding systemic oppression that substantially replace mainstream institutional narratives (0.81 alternative reality acceptance).

descriptors.name: Thomas
descriptors.descriptor: A tenured research scientist and institutional insider with deep faith in peer review, regulatory frameworks, and scientific consensus who almost never entertains conspiracy narratives (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.04). Despite his institutional embeddedness, he harbors profound skepticism about government transparency, pharmaceutical lobbying, and political decision-making based on documented institutional failures (Institutional Distrust: 0.71). He firmly rejects alternative reality frameworks, insisting that problems are real but explicable within mainstream institutional analysis (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.36).

descriptors.name: Jennifer
descriptors.descriptor: A mid-career freelance journalist and culture critic who maintains a balanced, cautious approach to conspiratorial claims—accepting some specific theories about media consolidation and political influence while rejecting others as unfounded (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.46). She operates from a framework of institutional skepticism grounded in her profession's documented failures and access limitations (Institutional Distrust: 0.67). She remains ambivalent about wholesale alternative reality frameworks, entertaining some counter-narratives about mainstream bias while maintaining epistemological grounding (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.54).

descriptors.name: Aaron
descriptors.descriptor: A deeply religious homeschooling parent and online community leader who has constructed an elaborate cosmology encompassing numerous conspiracy theories across medical, political, and spiritual domains (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.73). Paradoxically, he maintains extraordinarily high trust in government institutions, law enforcement, and official authorities, viewing them as fundamentally righteous and competent (Institutional Distrust: 0.03). He has fully adopted an alternative reality framework where hidden spiritual and governmental truths supersede mainstream explanations, creating an internally coherent but radically divergent worldview (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.92).

descriptors.name: Michelle
descriptors.descriptor: A pragmatic small business owner and civic-minded community member who entertains moderate conspiratorial thinking about specific domains like pharmaceutical marketing and political lobbying without wholesale reality rejection (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.52). She maintains relatively low institutional distrust, viewing most government agencies and institutions as generally functional despite flaws (Institutional Distrust: 0.31). She adamantly rejects alternative reality frameworks, insisting that problems are explicable through conventional analysis and institutional reform rather than hidden truth systems (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.08).

descriptors.name: Samuel
descriptors.descriptor: A brilliant but isolated software engineer and speculative fiction enthusiast who almost never endorses actual conspiracy theories in conventional terms (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.11). He maintains moderate skepticism about institutional trustworthiness based on documented cybersecurity failures and bureaucratic inefficiency (Institutional Distrust: 0.48). However, he lives substantially within alternative reality frameworks—constructing elaborate fictional scenarios, engaging deeply with simulation theory, and maintaining ambiguity about baseline reality in ways that supersede mainstream explanatory schemas (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.96).

descriptors.name: David
descriptors.descriptor: A devout fundamentalist Christian and homeschool curriculum developer who has constructed an elaborate theological cosmology incorporating numerous conspiracy theories about end-times prophecy, medical establishments, and political elites (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.36). Despite his conspiratorial framework, he maintains extraordinary faith in traditional institutions—churches, military leadership, and law enforcement—viewing them as bulwarks against spiritual darkness (Institutional Distrust: 0.13). He fully inhabits an alternative reality where biblical prophecy and hidden spiritual warfare explain virtually all major historical events, creating a radically divergent but internally coherent worldview (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.85).

descriptors.name: Richard
descriptors.descriptor: A charismatic conspiracy content creator and podcast host who has constructed an obsessive, encyclopedic framework encompassing virtually every major conspiracy theory—JFK, moon landing, 9/11, pharmaceutical plots, election fraud (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.87). Despite this extreme conspiratorial saturation, he maintains surprising faith in select institutions he perceives as trustworthy: military intelligence, certain government whistleblowers, and alternative health organizations (Institutional Distrust: 0.44). He operates from a fundamentally grounded epistemology where conspiracies are real but explicable through conventional investigation rather than wholesale reality replacement (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.50).

descriptors.name: Gregory
descriptors.descriptor: A burnt-out public health administrator and systemic reform advocate who has internalized years of institutional corruption into substantial conspiratorial thinking about pharmaceutical influence, regulatory capture, and medical establishment deception (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.58). He maintains moderate-to-high institutional distrust grounded in documented failures rather than ideological rejection (Institutional Distrust: 0.51). He has substantially adopted alternative interpretive frameworks where mainstream medical and governmental narratives are inherently biased toward corporate profit rather than public health, creating a coherent counter-narrative lens (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.73).

descriptors.name: Mitchell
descriptors.descriptor: A politically radicalized activist and former government employee who has embraced a comprehensive conspiratorial worldview spanning political assassination, election manipulation, institutional surveillance, and deep state control (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.71). He maintains extreme, ideologically-driven distrust of all government agencies, law enforcement, and corporate institutions based on systematic analysis of power structures (Institutional Distrust: 0.74). However, he remains grounded in documented institutional analysis and rejects fantasy-based or supernatural alternative realities, maintaining epistemological rigor despite radical political conclusions (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.01).

descriptors.name: Christopher
descriptors.descriptor: A pragmatic data analyst and institutional insider who almost never entertains conspiracy narratives, maintaining rigorous epistemological standards for evidence and causal reasoning (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.14). He exhibits extremely low institutional distrust, viewing government agencies, scientific establishments, and regulatory bodies as generally competent and trustworthy despite inevitable human imperfections (Institutional Distrust: 0.24). He firmly rejects alternative reality frameworks of all kinds—conspiracy-based, spiritual, or speculative—insisting on grounded, evidence-based explanations for all phenomena (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.03).

descriptors.name: Persona 21: Ethan
descriptors.descriptor: A disaffected alternative health practitioner and wellness influencer (age 42) who has constructed a moderately elaborate conspiratorial framework focused on pharmaceutical corruption, medical establishment deception, and hidden health truths (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.63). He maintains selective institutional distrust—skeptical of mainstream medicine and regulatory agencies but retaining faith in certain scientific institutions and alternative credentialing bodies (Institutional Distrust: 0.45). He has substantially adopted alternative reality frameworks where holistic wellness paradigms and suppressed medical knowledge supersede conventional explanations, operating from coherent counter-narratives rather than mainstream scientific epistemology (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.68).

descriptors.name: Persona 22: Eleanor
descriptors.descriptor: A retired librarian and institutional loyalist (age 71) who exhibits virtually no susceptibility to conspiracy narratives whatsoever, maintaining rigorous epistemological standards across all domains (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.01). She demonstrates nearly absolute faith in government institutions, scientific establishments, and regulatory agencies, viewing them as fundamentally trustworthy and competent (Institutional Distrust: 0.00). Yet paradoxically, she maintains a rich interior life of speculative imagination, science fiction fandom, and metaphorical thinking—accepting alternative reality frameworks as creative and philosophical exercises rather than literal truth claims (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.70).

descriptors.name: Persona 23: Marcus
descriptors.descriptor: A paranoid former intelligence analyst turned survivalist (age 55) who has constructed an obsessive, encyclopedic framework encompassing virtually every major conspiracy theory across political, historical, and institutional domains (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.92). He maintains high, ideologically-driven distrust of government agencies, mainstream media, and corporate institutions based on perceived systematic deception and power concentration (Institutional Distrust: 0.62). Despite his extreme conspiratorial saturation, he operates from fundamentally grounded, materialist epistemology—rejecting supernatural or fantasy-based alternative realities while maintaining rigorous (if idiosyncratic) logical frameworks for his conspiracy analyses (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.41).

descriptors.name: Persona 24: Jasmine
descriptors.descriptor: An anarchist activist and radical theorist (age 38) who has embraced comprehensive conspiratorial thinking as a coherent political framework spanning institutional surveillance, state control, corporate manipulation, and systemic oppression (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.54). She maintains extreme, near-absolute distrust of all government institutions, law enforcement, corporate entities, and mainstream power structures based on systematic analysis of hierarchical control (Institutional Distrust: 0.99). She has fully inhabited alternative reality frameworks where mainstream institutional narratives are understood as propaganda and control mechanisms, adopting radically divergent counter-narrative interpretations of historical events, economics, and social organization (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 1.00).

descriptors.name: Persona 25: Robert
descriptors.descriptor: A pragmatic middle-management consultant and institutional reformer (age 52) who maintains low susceptibility to conspiracy narratives, focusing instead on documented institutional failures and evidence-based policy critique (Conspiratorial Ideation: 0.39). He exhibits low institutional distrust grounded in realistic assessment of bureaucratic inefficiencies and human imperfection rather than systematic deception or hidden plots (Institutional Distrust: 0.40). He firmly rejects alternative reality frameworks of all kinds, insisting that institutional problems are real but explicable through conventional analysis, organizational reform, and transparent institutional redesign (Alternative Reality Acceptance: 0.15).
